title: Face processing EEG dataset for EEGLAB
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 18 participants performing a face processing task involving perceptual judgments of famous, unfamiliar, and scrambled faces. EEG and MEG data were simultaneously acquired in one session, while structural MRI was acquired in a separate session to support source localization. Data were collected across two recording sessions three months apart. Each face was presented twice with either immediate or delayed (5-15 presentations) repetition. This is a repackaged version of the original ds000117 dataset, with EEG data extracted from MEG/EEG files, resampled to 250 Hz, annotated with HED, and saved in EEGLAB format following minimal preprocessing to support investigation of neural responses to face categorization and repetition effects.
methods_description: Eighteen participants completed two recording sessions spaced three months apart. One session simultaneously recorded MEG and EEG data during a perceptual task; structural MRI was acquired in a separate session to support source localization. During each session, participants made symmetry judgments on presented face photographs (famous, unfamiliar, scrambled). Each face was presented twice with either immediate or delayed (5-15 presentations) repetition. EEG data were extracted from MEG/EEG fif files, resampled to 250 Hz, annotated with HED (v8.0.0), and saved in EEGLAB .set format. Minimal preprocessing included channel extraction, fiducial addition, channel renaming, event extraction and correction, and removal of spurious events.
